04

Clearances & EIA

Streamlining the regulatory pathway for industrial and infrastructure projects. We manage the complete environmental clearance lifecycle, from baseline data collection to final approval.

Get Clearance Audit

What's Included

End-to-end regulatory support for major projects.

CGWA / SGWA · REGULATORY

Groundwater Permission (CGWA)

Securing mandatory No Objection Certificates (NOC) from the Central Ground Water Authority for industrial, infrastructure, and mining projects.

  • Hydrogeological report preparation
  • Impact assessment on water table
  • Rainwater harvesting (RWH) design
  • Digital application & liaison
  • Annual compliance monitoring
Learn More & Case Studies →
SEIAA / MoEFCC · APPROVALS

Environmental Clearance (SEIAA / MoEF)

Managing Category A and Category B project clearances through State and Central expert appraisal committees (EAC/SEAC) with expert technical representation.

  • Form-1 & Form-2 documentation
  • Presentation for SEAC/EAC members
  • Baseline environment data audit
  • Public hearing management support
  • Six-monthly compliance reporting
Learn More & Case Studies →
EIA NOTIFICATION · SCIENTIFIC

Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA)

Developing comprehensive EIA reports that quantify the environmental impact of proposed developments across air, water, soil, and biodiversity.

  • Rapid & Comprehensive EIA study
  • Environment Management Plan (EMP)
  • Risk assessment & disaster modeling
  • Biodiversity impact studies
  • Socio-economic impact assessment
Learn More & Case Studies →
COMPLIANCE · AMENDMENTS

EC Renewals & Amendments

Managing the extension of validity and amendments in Environmental Clearance due to expansion, modernization, or changes in product mix.

  • Extension of Validity (EoV) apps
  • Amendment in TOR/EC requests
  • Compliance verification audits
  • Post-EC monitoring & reporting
  • Technical due diligence reports
Learn More & Case Studies →

Regulatory roadblocks

Delays in clearances can cost millions in project overhead.

01

Technical Gaps

Incomplete baseline data or flawed air/water modeling in EIA reports leads to immediate rejection by expert appraisal committees.

02

Procedural Delays

The SPCB and MoEFCC clearance cycles are complex. Mismanaging the submission timeline can cause months of unnecessary downtime.

03

Post-EC Compliance

Getting the clearance is only half the battle. Maintaining the mandatory six-monthly compliance reports is critical to avoid legal action.

The Clearance Protocol

A technical path from project planning to operation.

01

Scoping

  • Category Screening
  • Baseline Monitoring
  • Public Consultation
02

Modeling

  • Air Dispersion
  • Water Balance
  • Noise Mapping
03

Liaison

  • SPCB Submissions
  • Technical Defense
  • Public Hearings
04

Clearance

  • EC Procurement
  • CTE/CTO Grants
  • Compliance Audit

Regulatory Assets

Full documentation suite for legal compliance.

EIA Reports
EMP Strategies
6-Monthly Compliance
Audit Ready CTO

Regulatory Frameworks

Navigation through MoEFCC and SPCB clearance protocols.

EIA Categorization
  • Category A MoEFCC (Central) Clearance
  • Category B1 SEIAA (State) with Public Hearing
  • Category B2 SEIAA (State) exempt from EIA/PH
  • EIA Notification 2006 (and subsequent amendments)
Standard Clearances
  • CTE / CTO Water & Air Act Consents (SPCB)
  • CGWA NOC Groundwater abstraction permits
  • Coastal CRZ Clearance for marine/coastal projects
  • Post-EC Compliance Six-monthly monitoring reports

Common Questions

Guidance on environmental regulatory compliance.

The timeline varies based on the project category (A or B). Generally, Category A projects at the central level take 8 to 12 months, while Category B projects at the state level take 6 to 9 months, including public hearings and technical appraisal committee reviews.
Public Hearing is mandatory for most Category A and Category B1 projects as per the EIA Notification 2006. It ensures local stakeholders and communities have a platform to voice environmental concerns before the project is approved.
Yes, if your site abstracts groundwater for industrial and commercial use, a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) is mandatory. Penalties for non-compliance can include sealing of borewells and heavy environmental compensation fines.
MoEFCC Approved QCI Accredited CGWA Specialist SPCB Liaison
